speaker: DUCHESS
text: Why droops my lord like over-ripened corn

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Hanging the head at Ceres’ plenteous load?

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Why doth the great Duke Humphrey knit his brows,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: As frowning at the favors of the world?

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Why are thine eyes fixed to the sullen earth,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Gazing on that which seems to dim thy sight?

speaker: DUCHESS
text: What seest thou there? King Henry’s diadem,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Enchased with all the honors of the world?

speaker: DUCHESS
text: If so, gaze on and grovel on thy face

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Until thy head be circled with the same.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Put forth thy hand; reach at the glorious gold.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: What, is ’t too short? I’ll lengthen it with mine;

speaker: DUCHESS
text: And, having both together heaved it up,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: We’ll both together lift our heads to heaven

speaker: DUCHESS
text: And never more abase our sight so low

speaker: DUCHESS
text: As to vouchsafe one glance unto the ground.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: O Nell, sweet Nell, if thou dost love thy lord,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Banish the canker of ambitious thoughts!

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: And may that hour when I imagine ill

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Against my king and nephew, virtuous Henry,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Be my last breathing in this mortal world!

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: My troublous dreams this night doth make me sad.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: What dreamed my lord? Tell me, and I’ll requite it

speaker: DUCHESS
text: With sweet rehearsal of my morning’s dream.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Methought this staff, mine office badge in court,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Was broke in twain—by whom I have forgot,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: But, as I think, it was by th’ Cardinal—

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: And on the pieces of the broken wand

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Were placed the heads of Edmund, Duke of Somerset,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: And William de la Pole, first Duke of Suffolk.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: This was my dream. What it doth bode God knows.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Tut, this was nothing but an argument

speaker: DUCHESS
text: That he that breaks a stick of Gloucester’s grove

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Shall lose his head for his presumption.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: But list to me, my Humphrey, my sweet duke:

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Methought I sat in seat of majesty,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: In the cathedral church of Westminster

speaker: DUCHESS
text: And in that chair where kings and queens were crowned,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Where Henry and Dame Margaret kneeled to me

speaker: DUCHESS
text: And on my head did set the diadem.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Nay, Eleanor, then must I chide outright.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Presumptuous dame, ill-nurtured Eleanor,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Art thou not second woman in the realm

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: And the Protector’s wife, beloved of him?

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Hast thou not worldly pleasure at command,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Above the reach or compass of thy thought?

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: And wilt thou still be hammering treachery

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: To tumble down thy husband and thyself

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: From top of honor to disgrace’s feet?

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Away from me, and let me hear no more!

speaker: DUCHESS
text: What, what, my lord? Are you so choleric

speaker: DUCHESS
text: With Eleanor for telling but her dream?

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Next time I’ll keep my dreams unto myself

speaker: DUCHESS
text: And not be checked.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Nay, be not angry. I am pleased again.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: My Lord Protector, ’tis his Highness’ pleasure

speaker: MESSENGER
text: You do prepare to ride unto Saint Albans,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Whereas the King and Queen do mean to hawk.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: I go.—Come, Nell, thou wilt ride with us?

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Yes, my good lord. I’ll follow presently.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Follow I must; I cannot go before

speaker: DUCHESS
text: While Gloucester bears this base and humble mind.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Were I a man, a duke, and next of blood,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: I would remove these tedious stumbling blocks

speaker: DUCHESS
text: And smooth my way upon their headless necks;

speaker: DUCHESS
text: And, being a woman, I will not be slack

speaker: DUCHESS
text: To play my part in Fortune’s pageant.—

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Where are you there? Sir John! Nay, fear not, man.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: We are alone; here’s none but thee and I.

speaker: HUME
text: Jesus preserve your royal Majesty!

speaker: DUCHESS
text: What sayst thou? “Majesty”? I am but “Grace.”

speaker: HUME
text: But by the grace of God and Hume’s advice,

speaker: HUME
text: Your Grace’s title shall be multiplied.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: What sayst thou, man? Hast thou as yet conferred

speaker: DUCHESS
text: With Margery Jourdain, the cunning witch,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: With Roger Bolingbroke, the conjurer?

speaker: DUCHESS
text: And will they undertake to do me good?

speaker: HUME
text: This they have promisèd: to show your Highness

speaker: HUME
text: A spirit raised from depth of underground

speaker: HUME
text: That shall make answer to such questions

speaker: HUME
text: As by your Grace shall be propounded him.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: It is enough. I’ll think upon the questions.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: When from Saint Albans we do make return,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: We’ll see these things effected to the full.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Here, Hume, take this reward.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Make merry, man,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: With thy confederates in this weighty cause.

speaker: HUME
text: Hume must make merry with the Duchess’ gold.

speaker: HUME
text: Marry, and shall! But, how now, Sir John Hume?

speaker: HUME
text: Seal up your lips, and give no words but “mum”;

speaker: HUME
text: The business asketh silent secrecy.

speaker: HUME
text: Dame Eleanor gives gold to bring the witch;

speaker: HUME
text: Gold cannot come amiss, were she a devil.

speaker: HUME
text: Yet have I gold flies from another coast—

speaker: HUME
text: I dare not say, from the rich cardinal

speaker: HUME
text: And from the great and new-made Duke of Suffolk,

speaker: HUME
text: Yet I do find it so. For, to be plain,

speaker: HUME
text: They, knowing Dame Eleanor’s aspiring humor,

speaker: HUME
text: Have hirèd me to undermine the Duchess

speaker: HUME
text: And buzz these conjurations in her brain.

speaker: HUME
text: They say a crafty knave does need no broker,

speaker: HUME
text: Yet am I Suffolk and the Cardinal’s broker.

speaker: HUME
text: Hume, if you take not heed, you shall go near

speaker: HUME
text: To call them both a pair of crafty knaves.

speaker: HUME
text: Well, so it stands; and thus I fear at last

speaker: HUME
text: Hume’s knavery will be the Duchess’ wrack,

speaker: HUME
text: And her attainture will be Humphrey’s fall.

speaker: HUME
text: Sort how it will, I shall have gold for all.
